─[1]-Project──
lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ OpenCode Support ]─ ┐

OpenCode Support

Add OpenCode as a second supported platform. All work happens in the lore source repo and create-lore repo.

Design Decisions

CLAUDE.md, not AGENTS.md. OpenCode’s opencode.json has an instructions array that can point to any file. Point it at ["CLAUDE.md", "MEMORY.local.md"]. One canonical instructions file serves both platforms. No duplication, no generation step.

No delegation-enforcer. The legacy version requires hardcoded domain-to-executable mappings (terraform, kubectl, etc.) that are project-specific. The knowledge-tracker already provides soft delegation reminders. Operators can add their own enforcer if they want hard blocking. Skip for template.

Shared lib architecture. Core logic lives in lib/ (CJS). Both Claude Code hooks and OpenCode plugins are thin adapters over the same shared functions. createRequire(import.meta.url) bridges ESM plugins to CJS lib. Adding future platforms (Cursor, etc.) only requires a new adapter per hook.

Aligned filenames. Both platforms use the same names: session-init, knowledge-tracker, protect-memory. Originally the OpenCode plugins had different names (principles-injector) — renamed for cross-platform clarity.

Template ships both platforms. Every Lore project gets both hooks/ (Claude Code) and .opencode/ (OpenCode). Unused platform files are inert. No installer flags, no selective cleanup, no upgrade path problems.

Steps

1. Write OpenCode plugins and shared lib

  • Extract shared logic into lib/banner.js, lib/tracker.js, lib/memory-guard.js
  • Create .opencode/plugins/ with three ESM adapters: session-init.js, knowledge-tracker.js, protect-memory.js
  • Create .opencode/package.json with @opencode-ai/plugin dependency and "type": "module"
  • Create opencode.json pointing to ["CLAUDE.md", "MEMORY.local.md"]
  • Refactor Claude Code hooks to thin CJS adapters importing from lib/
  • Rename post-edit-reminder.js to knowledge-tracker.js on both platforms

2. Write tests

  • 23 new tests in test/opencode-plugins.test.js covering all three plugins
  • Updated test/session-init.test.js for shared lib setup
  • All 69 tests pass in lore repo

3. Update create-lore tests

  • Added assertions verifying .opencode/plugins/ (3 files) and opencode.json exist in scaffolded projects
  • All 8 tests pass in create-lore repo

4. Lingering items

  • YAML parsing constraint documented in comments (single-line values only)
  • Escalation thresholds configurable via nudgeThreshold/warnThreshold in .lore-config (defaults: 3, 5)
  • Thresholds work in both Claude Code hook and OpenCode plugin

5. README honesty pass

  • Two-platform quick-start (Claude Code + OpenCode)
  • Before/after section
  • Supported platforms table
  • Updated component table to include plugins

6. End-to-end verification

  • Scaffold a test project with npx create-lore
  • Open in Claude Code, verify session banner + hooks fire
  • Open in OpenCode, verify session banner + plugins fire
  • Same knowledge base, two platforms, one repo

7. Publish

  • Tag lore repo (v0.3.0)
  • Bump create-lore version (v0.3.0)
  • Publish to npm
  • Sync to lorehq instance

Architecture

lib/
  banner.js        <- buildBanner(), ensureStickyFiles(), getAgentDomains()
  tracker.js       <- processToolUse(), getThresholds(), nav-dirty helpers
  memory-guard.js  <- checkMemoryAccess()

hooks/                          .opencode/plugins/
  session-init.js      <->        session-init.js
  knowledge-tracker.js <->        knowledge-tracker.js
  protect-memory.js    <->        protect-memory.js
       |                              |
       +---- both import from lib/ ---+

Success Criteria

  • OpenCode plugins produce same session context as Claude Code hooks
  • npx create-lore scaffolds working dual-platform project
  • All tests pass (69 lore + 8 create-lore)
  • README reflects two-platform reality
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
